event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit, ornate room, Master Oogway stirs a bowl, from which a glowing yin-yang symbol emerges, as a narrator recounts Shen's parents consulting a soothsayer who foretold his defeat by a warrior of black and white, a prophecy Shen's actions only sealed. The scene shifts to a fiery, red-hued environment where Po and a red-eyed creature run amidst intensifying music, with humorous text overlays commenting on pandas and Oogway's symbolism. Master Oogway wisely states, "One often meets his destiny on the road he takes to avoid it." Shen returns to his parents, who react with horror, as the scene shows a dragon-shaped window, concerned peacocks, stirring sounds, a metallic clang, and a menacing growl. In a dark courtyard, Tai Lung strides confidently with loud grunts and heavy impacts, his menacing presence underscored by roars, while text overlays mock his entrance. A white peacock character, displaying arrogance, approaches Shen in a grand courtyard, demanding he leave. Shen, stern, questions "Your house?" to which the peacock points to a peacock on the door. The scene widens to a vast courtyard under a blood-red sky, where Shen, flanked by soldiers, dismisses the peacock's fortune-telling skills. The peacock dramatically bows, fanning his feathers, and addresses Shen, "We shall see, Shen." Shen insults him, "Oh, you shall see, old goat." Shen asserts, "Gongmen is under the stewardship of the Master's Council and we will protect it even from you." The peacock mockingly laughs and asks about a "gift," revealing it's a "parting gift" that will "part you." As the peacock strikes a pose, Shen roars and charges, accompanied by sounds of impact and struggle. The video then shows the white peacock engaging in acrobatic martial arts against Shen in a red-lit courtyard. In a throne room, the peacock taunts, "Show off," and reminisces about his father's throne. Shen looms menacingly as the peacock lunges with a blade, taunting, "Greetings, Panda. At last we meet." A deeper voice off-screen says, "A little to the left. Oh, but it's so heavy, master." The peacock declares, "Thirty years I've waited for this moment. Everything must be exactly how I..." as ironic on-screen text appears. The scene concludes with the peacock defiantly standing, flanked by two large, dark, gorilla-like figures.

Red-Eyed Creature.description: A small, red-eyed animal seen running alongside Po in a fiery environment, adding to the sense of danger or urgency.

Dragon-Shaped Window.description: An ornate window with a dragon design, backlit by a red sun, symbolizing a shift in atmosphere to something more ominous or foreboding.

Master Oogway.description: A wise, turtle-like character wearing a traditional hat, seen stirring a bowl from which a glowing yin-yang symbol emerges, symbolizing balance and wisdom. He is heard speaking sagely, 'One often meets his destiny on the road he takes to avoid it.'

Peacock Figures.description: A white peacock with a long neck and ornate, eye-patterned feathers, displaying theatrical arrogance and intense focus, wearing a simple sash, and wielding a sharp blade while performing acrobatic martial arts. He possesses a deep, resonant, and taunting voice, speaking lines like "Show off," reminiscing about "My father's throne," and addressing his opponent with "Greetings, Panda. At last we meet," and "Thirty years I've waited for this moment. Everything must be exactly how I..."

Po.description: A panda character, Po, shown running in a fiery, red-lit environment, suggesting urgency or escape. He is implicitly linked to a prophecy of a 'warrior of black and white' by a female narrator, a detail humorously reinforced by on-screen text.

Tai Lung.description: A large, horned boar-like creature with a menacing presence, wearing a red sash, standing atop a platform, surrounded by soldiers, and displaying intense anger and aggression.

Shen.description: A large, horned boar-like creature with a red sash, shown looming menacingly in the shadows and then fully revealed in an imposing stance. He is addressed as "Panda" by the peacock and emits a guttural sound during the confrontation.

Gorilla-Like Figures.description: Two large, dark, gorilla-like creatures with fierce expressions, standing flanking the peacock in the throne room, suggesting they are his allies or enforcers; they wear dark armor and appear intimidating, adding to the scene’s tension.

raw_segments: The video opens with a stylized, shadow-puppet-like scene in a dimly lit, ornate room with greenish lighting, featuring two peacock-like figures. A wise, turtle-like character, Master Oogway, is seen stirring a bowl, from which a glowing yin-yang symbol emerges, symbolizing balance and harmony. A female narrator's voice begins, recounting how "Shen's troubled parents consulted a soothsayer," who "foretold that if Shen continued down this dark path, he would be defeated by a warrior of black and white." Dramatic music swells as the narrator continues, explaining how "the young lord set out to change his fate, but what he did next only sealed it." The scene transitions to a fiery, red-hued environment where a panda, Po, is seen running with a group of animals, including a red-eyed creature, suggesting urgency or danger, as the music intensifies. Text overlays humorously comment on pandas being the 'ONLY black and white animal' and Oogway 'cooking with this line,' referencing the yin-yang symbolism. Master Oogway's wise, male voice then states, "One often meets his destiny on the road he takes to avoid it." The female narrator returns, describing how "Shen returned to his parents full of pride, but in their faces, he saw only horror." The scene shifts to a close-up of a dragon-shaped window with a red sun behind it, then to a pair of peacocks with concerned expressions, accompanied by the sound of stirring, followed by a distinct metallic clang and a menacing growl. The setting changes to a dark, ominous courtyard with traditional Chinese architecture, where a large, horned boar-like creature, Tai Lung, strides confidently with a menacing presence, his powerful movements underscored by loud grunts, roars, and heavy impacts. Text overlays mock his entrance with 'meow, fuck out the way' and 'That's the stride of someone who knows he's all that.' The final shot shows a close-up of Tai Lung’s face, revealing his intense, focused eyes and sharp features, as more impactful sounds emphasize his threat while he approaches the camera.

The video opens with a white peacock character, adorned with a long neck and a fan of feathers featuring eye-like patterns, walking confidently toward a large stone archway in a grand ancient courtyard. The peacock, displaying an air of arrogance and speaking with a distinct, somewhat effeminate and mocking voice, approaches a large, horned boar-like creature, identified as Shen. The peacock greets, 'Good afternoon, gentlemen,' and then arrogantly demands, 'Please leave my house.' Shen, wearing a red sash and exhibiting a stern expression, responds with a deep, authoritative voice, 'Your house?' The peacock retorts, 'Yes, didn't you see the peacock on the front door?' The scene shifts to a wider view of a vast courtyard under a blood-red sky, where Shen stands atop a platform flanked by soldiers. Shen dismisses the peacock's 'fortune-telling skills' as 'not as good as you thought.' Unfazed, the peacock performs a dramatic, theatrical bow, spreading his feathers wide in a display of vanity, and addresses him directly, 'We shall see, Shen.' Shen, his expression hardening, insults the peacock, 'Oh, you shall see, old goat.' After the peacock asks, 'Where were we?', Shen asserts, 'Gongmen is under the stewardship of the Master's Council and we will protect it even from you.' The peacock laughs mockingly, stating, 'I'm so glad you feel that way,' and then asks Shen, 'What's in the box, Shen?' He then reveals, 'It's a gift,' elaborating with a chilling threat that it's a 'parting gift' that will 'part you,' leaving 'part of you here, part of you there, and part of you way over there, staining the wall!' As the peacock strikes a flamboyant pose, his feathers fanned out, Shen roars in anger and charges forward, accompanied by sounds of impact, struggle, and grunts, under the ominous red sky. Comedic on-screen text, such as 'please', 'yesssss', 'door?', and 'gift :)', enhances the absurdity of the escalating confrontation.

The minute opens with the chaotic sounds of clashing metal and combat as a white peacock, adorned with ornate, eye-patterned feathers, engages in acrobatic martial arts against a large, horned boar-like creature identified as Shen, in a grand, red-lit courtyard. The peacock flips and dodges with agility amidst traditional Chinese architecture. The scene then shifts to a dimly lit throne room where the peacock, with a deep, resonant voice, taunts, "Show off." Dramatic, slightly ominous music swells as he stands confidently, then turns to face Shen, who looms menacingly in the shadows. The peacock continues, reflecting on "My father's throne," and reminiscing, "He used to let me play here beside him, promising someday this throne would be mine." As Shen’s imposing form fully emerges, the peacock performs a dramatic spin, feathers fanning out, before lunging forward with a blade, addressing Shen with a taunting, "Greetings, Panda. At last we meet." A guttural sound, possibly from Shen, is heard, followed by the peacock's continued taunts: "We meet at last! Yes, that's it! Greetings, Panda. We meet!" A different, deeper voice is heard saying, "A little to the left. Oh, but it's so heavy, master," suggesting an off-screen action. The peacock, with an intense expression, declares, "Thirty years I've waited for this moment. Everything must be exactly how I..." as on-screen text like 'This shit is nice.', 'Do better.', and 'exactly' appears, adding a modern, ironic commentary. The scene concludes with the peacock standing defiantly, flanked by two large, dark, gorilla-like figures.

event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit, ornate throne room accented with red and gold, a white peacock, adorned with a sash and eye-patterned feathers, directs a voice-acting session with a male voice stating, "I envisioned it a little to the left... perfect" and "With the weapon by my... a little bit more." The peacock taunts two large, dark gorilla-like figures who respond with exclamations like "Watch it!" and "I saw a panda," accompanied by grunts and growls. The peacock, displaying arrogance, gestures dramatically, his expression shifting from smug to intense, exclaiming "Oh, come on" and "Greetings, Panda." He performs a swift, acrobatic martial arts move, drawing a sharp blade with precision, his confident demeanor captured closely, culminating in a satisfied "Yes! That's it!" The peacock then turns to face Shen, a large, horned boar-like creature standing ominously in the shadows, who declares "At last, we meet" and "He arrives in chains." As the peacock's expression hardens, Shen looms with authority. A female voice echoes, "Yes, all is well," prompting Shen to question, "What do you mean all is well?" and "What did you mean all is well from my point of view or all is well from your point of view?" Multiple voices then repeat "Well" in various tones, indicating a voice acting session. On-screen text appears intermittently, including 'And', 'Dumbasses.', 'Yes! :D', and 'Deleted scenes, my beloveds.', along with credits for 'Ares Deveaux, CHARACTER ANIMATOR'. The scene concludes with the peacock in a defiant pose, blade raised, facing Shen, with the gorillas still guarding.

On-Screen Text.description: Intermittent white text overlays such as 'And', 'Dumbasses.', 'Yes! :D', and 'Deleted scenes, my beloveds.', along with a credit for 'Ares Deveaux, CHARACTER ANIMATOR', indicating this is a behind-the-scenes or deleted scene compilation.

Dragon-Shaped Window.description: An ornate window with a dragon design, backlit by a red sun, symbolizing a shift in atmosphere to something more ominous or foreboding.

Shen.description: A large, horned boar-like creature with a red sash, standing on a raised platform, surrounded by soldiers, and exhibiting a mix of anger and authority; his horns are prominent and curled, and his expression shifts from stern to enraged; in this segment, he is shown looming in the shadows, then fully revealed in a menacing stance, confronting the peacock, and is voiced by a deep, authoritative male actor who declares "At last, we meet," discusses a prisoner arriving "in chains," and questions the meaning of "all is well."

Peacock Figures.description: A white peacock with a long neck and ornate, eye-patterned feathers, displaying arrogance and theatricality, wearing a simple sash, and engaging in taunting gestures and poses; in this segment, he wields a sharp blade and performs acrobatic martial arts moves with intense focus and confidence, voiced by a male actor who directs or critiques with lines like "I envisioned it a little to the left... perfect," expresses frustration with "Oh, come on," greets with "Greetings, Panda," and shows satisfaction with "Yes! That's it!"

Gorilla-Like Figures.description: Two large, dark, gorilla-like creatures with fierce expressions, standing flanking the peacock in the throne room, suggesting they are his allies or enforcers; they wear dark armor and appear intimidating, with one or more male voices exclaiming "Watch it!", "I saw a panda," and "A panda!", followed by grunts and growls.

raw_segments: The scene opens in a dimly lit, ornate throne room with red and gold accents, featuring intricate Chinese-style lattice windows backlit by a warm, glowing light. A white peacock, adorned with a simple sash and eye-patterned feathers, stands confidently, initially appearing to direct or critique with a male voice stating, "I envisioned it a little to the left... perfect" and "With the weapon by my... a little bit more." He then taunts two large, dark gorilla-like figures flanking him, who respond with exclamations like "Watch it!", "I saw a panda," and "A panda!", followed by grunts and growls. The peacock, displaying arrogance and theatricality, gestures dramatically while speaking, his expression shifting from smug to intense, even exclaiming "Oh, come on" and "Greetings, Panda." He performs a swift, acrobatic martial arts move, drawing a sharp blade with precision and focus, his confident demeanor and sharp expressions captured closely by the camera, culminating in a satisfied "Yes! That's it!" As the scene progresses, the peacock turns to face a large, horned boar-like creature—Shen—standing ominously in the shadows, his red sash and curled horns visible. Shen, voiced by a deep, authoritative male actor, declares "At last, we meet" and "We at last meet," later adding, "He arrives in chains" and "So, all is well." The peacock’s expression hardens as he confronts Shen, who looms with authority and menace. A female voice then echoes, "Yes, all is well," leading to Shen questioning, "What do you mean all is well?" and elaborating, "What did you mean all is well from my point of view or all is well from your point of view?" Multiple voices, both male and female, then repeat "Well" in various tones, suggesting a voice acting session. The atmosphere grows tense, underscored by the ornate surroundings and the red-lit windows, which cast dramatic shadows. On-screen text appears intermittently, including 'And', 'Dumbasses.', 'Yes! :D', and 'Deleted scenes, my beloveds.', along with credits for 'Ares Deveaux, CHARACTER ANIMATOR', confirming the behind-the-scenes context. The peacock’s final pose is one of defiant readiness, blade raised, as he faces Shen, with the gorillas still standing guard.

event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit, opulent Chinese-style hall, Shen, a white peacock, dramatically unfurls his ornate, eye-patterned feathers with intense, percussive sounds and building dramatic music, accompanied by whooshes. Shen taunts the arrival of Po, who emerges from a doorway with a determined yet bewildered expression, muttering about an "evil janitor or something?" Shen greets Po with a calm, sinister voice, "Greetings, Panda. We meet...", to which Po casually responds, "Hey, how you doing?" Shen lets out a mocking "Heh," prompting Po to ask, "What's so funny?" Shen, with a condescending tone, replies, "A funny thing that you clearly do not know." Po, frustrated, rambles about the weapon being tiny then big, and a "lady over here, I think it's a guy..." before exclaiming "Whoa!" as Shen, now wielding a sharp blade, performs acrobatic martial arts moves with his feathers flaring. Shen, flanked by two silent, dark, gorilla-like enforcers, coldly states, "The only reason you are still alive is that I find your stupidity mildly amusing." As dramatic music swells, Po, with a dawning realization, asks, "You, you were there." Shen confirms with a chilling tone, "Yes. Yes, I was," followed by a whoosh and impact sound. Shen unleashes a red, fiery energy blast, sending Po flying backward amidst intense, orchestral music as the environment shifts to a darker, red-lit, smoky tone. On-screen text overlays like 'this thing is what I was fussing about..?' and 'Have fun with your trauma' add meta-commentary to the dramatic exchange, culminating in a close-up of a menacing red-eyed creature with glowing eyes.

Red-Eyed Creature.description: A small, red-eyed animal, possibly a wolf or fox, with glowing eyes, seen in a close-up at the end, suggesting a new threat or transformation, adding to the ominous atmosphere.

On-Screen Text.description: Intermittent white text overlays such as 'this thing is what I was fussing about..?', 'Born to 'we meet at last', forced to 'hey'', '*chuckles at repressed trauma*', 'Imagine having your traumatic memories repressed? Pfft, what a loser.', and 'Have fun with your trauma' with a hand emoji, adding humorous, meta-commentary to the dramatic scene.

Peacock Figures.description: A white peacock, identified as Shen, with a long neck and ornate, eye-patterned feathers, displaying arrogance and theatricality. He speaks with a calm, sinister, deep, and arrogant voice, mocking Po and confirming his presence at a past traumatic event. In this segment, he wields a sharp blade, performs acrobatic martial arts moves with intense focus and confidence, and later unleashes a red energy blast, accompanied by dramatic percussive sounds and whooshes.

Po.description: A panda character, shown emerging from a doorway with a determined yet slightly bewildered expression. He speaks with a casual, confused, then frustrated, and finally a realizing/traumatized tone, asking questions and expressing surprise. He reacts to Shen’s taunts with confusion and defiance, then is struck by a red energy blast and sent flying backward.

Gorilla-Like Figures.description: Two large, dark, gorilla-like creatures with fierce expressions, standing flanking the peacock in the throne room, suggesting they are his allies or enforcers; they wear dark armor and appear intimidating, adding to the scene’s tension.

raw_segments: The scene opens with a white peacock, identified as Shen, dramatically unfurling his ornate, eye-patterned feathers with a series of intense, percussive sounds and building dramatic music in a dimly lit, opulent Chinese-style hall. Shen stands confidently, his movements accompanied by whooshes, as he taunts the arrival of Po, who emerges from a doorway. Po, with a determined yet bewildered expression, initially mutters about an "evil janitor or something?" before Shen greets him with a calm, sinister voice, "Greetings, Panda. We meet..." Po responds casually, "Hey, how you doing?" Shen then lets out a mocking "Heh," prompting Po to ask, "What's so funny?" Shen, with a condescending tone, replies, "A funny thing that you clearly do not know." Po, frustrated, rambles, "Okay, enough with these riddles. Would you guys spill the beans? First, you know, we come in and the weapon is tiny. Surprise, it's big. And then the lady over here, I think it's a guy..." before exclaiming "Whoa!" as Shen, now wielding a sharp blade, performs acrobatic martial arts moves, his feathers flaring. Shen, flanked by two silent, dark, gorilla-like enforcers, then coldly states, "The only reason you are still alive is that I find your stupidity mildly amusing." As dramatic music swells, Po, with a dawning realization, asks, "You, you were there." Shen confirms with a chilling tone, "Yes. Yes, I was," followed by a whoosh and impact sound. The tension peaks as Shen unleashes a red, fiery energy blast, sending Po flying backward amidst intense, orchestral music. The environment shifts to a darker, red-lit, smoky tone, culminating in a close-up of a menacing red-eyed creature with glowing eyes, suggesting a new threat. On-screen text overlays like 'this thing is what I was fussing about..?' and 'Have fun with your trauma' add humorous, meta-commentary to the dramatic exchange.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a split-screen contrasting a white peacock, labeled 'little panda,' with a red-eyed wolf-like creature, 'kitty,' as Shen, in Spanish, commands 'Arre, arre, gatito,' met by the Peacock's rapid 'No, no, no, no, no.' Shen then orders, 'Call in the wolves, all of them! I want them ready to move!' The scene shifts to a dimly lit, fiery room where the peacock, armed with a blade, confronts Shen, a horned boar-like figure, amidst soldiers, while on-screen text notes 'pookie is upset. you hate to see it 😔.' The peacock dramatically proclaims, 'The year of the peacock begins now!' a statement questioned by a casual male voice offering a humorous 'Happy New Year, sir.' Shen, with a gravelly voice, reiterates, 'Get the wolves ready. We're loading the ships now,' before unleashing an enraged roar, visually contorting his face, which is met by the peacock's repeated 'No, no, no, no, no!' Shen calmly demands, 'Bring me everyone,' escalating to an enraged scream of 'Everyone!' after a questioning male voice asks, 'What do you mean everyone?' A live-action clip of '*Boss Wolf*' and '*Shen*' screaming in fury adds a meta-layer. Returning to the animated scene, the peacock unleashes a red energy blast from his blade, engulfing Shen in flames; as the explosion subsides, revealing a defeated Shen, a female voice accuses, 'You just destroyed your ancestral home, Shen.' Shen calmly responds, 'A trivial sacrifice, when all of China is my reward,' before the female voice questions his satisfaction. The atmosphere remains tense and dramatic, dominated by red lighting. The Peacock Figures, illuminated by a sinister red glow, utters 'my god...' with a smug expression, as Shen states, 'abjugation of the whole world finally make you feel better.' The Peacock Figures calmly suggests, 'This is a start. I might also convert the basement into a dungeon,' then confronts Shen, who looms menacingly. Shen urges, 'So your parents can rest in peace,' to which the Peacock Figures, with self-aware humor noted by on-screen text 'bros playing into it with self-aware jokes top-tier villain,' states, 'My parents hated me. Do you understand? They wronged me. And I will make it right.' Shen's voice softens, explaining, 'They loved you. They loved you so much that having to send you away killed them.' The Peacock Figures unfurls his eye-patterned feathers, confidently stating, 'The dead exist in the past. And I must attend to the future,' a sentiment noted by 'leaving this scene here because I still love that they gave him actual depth.' Shen growls, demanding, 'Tell me what happened that night!' The Peacock Figures feigns ignorance, asking 'What night?' Shen repeats 'That night!' prompting a sarcastic realization from the Peacock Figures, 'Ah, that night.' Shen urgently confirms, 'Yes! We're talking about the same night, right?' The Peacock Figures continues to taunt, 'And yes, I'm gonna eat up parallels EVERY time.' The Peacock Figures unleashes a red energy blast with a loud 'YAAAH!', striking Shen and sending him flying backward in a dramatic explosion of debris, accompanied by whooshing, impact sounds, and Shen's pained 'AHHH!'. The scene concludes with a close-up of the Peacock Figures, now a menacing figure with glowing red eyes and radiating power, as the text 'I am literally bitchless.' appears, underscoring his ruthless confidence in the dark, foreboding environment bathed in red light, with wooden structures and fire.

On-Screen Text.description: Intermittent white text overlays such as 'my god...', 'bros playing into it with self-aware jokes top-tier villain', 'leaving this scene here because I still love that they gave him actual depth', 'And yes, I'm gonna eat up parallels EVERY time', 'I am literally bitchless.', adding humorous, meta-commentary to the dramatic scene.

Shen.description: A large, horned boar-like creature with a red sash, standing on a raised platform, surrounded by soldiers, and exhibiting a mix of anger and authority; his horns are prominent and curled, and his expression shifts from stern to enraged; in this segment, he is shown looming in the shadows, then fully revealed in a menacing stance, confronting the peacock, and ultimately being struck by a red energy blast and sent flying backward. He speaks with a deep, authoritative, then softer and reasoning, and finally demanding and intense voice, and emits a growl and a pained 'AHHH!' when hit.

Peacock Figures.description: A white peacock with a long neck and ornate, eye-patterned feathers, displaying arrogance and theatricality, wearing a simple sash, and engaging in taunting gestures and poses; in this segment, he wields a sharp blade and performs acrobatic martial arts moves with intense focus and confidence, later unleashing a red energy blast and transforming into a menacing figure with glowing red eyes. He speaks with a calm, slightly mocking, then serious and pained, then dismissive and cold, and finally sarcastic tone, and makes a distinct 'YAAAH!' sound during his attack.

*Boss Wolf*.description: A live-action man with dark, slicked-back hair and a light-colored suit, labeled '*Boss Wolf*', who appears in a close-up, screaming in fury, adding a meta-reference to the animated confrontation with Shen.

*Shen* (Live-Action).description: A live-action man with dark, slicked-back hair and a light-colored suit, labeled '*Shen*', who appears in a close-up, screaming in fury, mirroring the animated Shen’s rage and adding a dramatic, real-world parallel to the animated conflict.

raw_segments: The video opens with a split-screen, visually presenting a white peacock labeled 'little panda' and a menacing red-eyed wolf-like creature labeled 'kitty.' A deep, growling voice, identified as Shen, addresses the 'kitty' in Spanish, 'Arre, arre, gatito,' followed by a more refined voice, the Peacock, responding with a rapid 'No, no, no, no, no.' A commanding voice, also Shen, then orders, 'Call in the wolves, all of them! I want them ready to move!' The scene quickly shifts to a dimly lit, fiery room where the peacock, now wielding a sharp blade, confronts Shen, a large, horned boar-like figure, standing atop a platform surrounded by soldiers. On-screen text reads 'pookie is upset. you hate to see it 😔.' The peacock, with theatrical flair, declares in a distinct voice, 'The year of the peacock begins now!' A casual male voice then interjects, questioning 'Right now?' and humorously explaining, 'Because it's the middle of the year, so you'd only get like a half of the year of the peacock,' before wishing 'Happy New Year, sir.' The tension escalates as a deep, gravelly voice (Shen) gives further orders: 'Get the wolves ready. We're loading the ships now.' Shen then unleashes a loud, enraged roar, visually contorting his face in rage, which is met by the peacock's rapid, high-pitched 'No, no, no, no, no!' A calm, authoritative voice (Shen) demands, 'Bring me everyone,' prompting a questioning male voice to ask, 'What do you mean everyone?' Shen's voice then escalates into an enraged scream of 'Everyone!' The scene cuts to a live-action clip of a man labeled '*Boss Wolf*' and '*Shen*', screaming in fury, adding a meta-layer. The video returns to the animated scene where the peacock unleashes a powerful red energy blast from his blade, engulfing Shen in flames. As the explosion subsides, revealing a smoldering, defeated Shen, a female voice accuses, 'You just destroyed your ancestral home, Shen.' Shen, with a calm, philosophical tone, responds, 'A trivial sacrifice, when all of China is my reward,' before the female voice questions, 'Then will you finally be satisfied? Will...' The atmosphere remains tense and dramatic, dominated by red lighting, emphasizing the conflict and the peacock’s dominance.

The scene opens with a close-up of the Peacock Figures, his face illuminated by a sinister red glow, as he utters 'my god...' with a smug, theatrical expression. A deep, authoritative voice, belonging to Shen, is heard stating, "abjugation of the whole world finally make you feel better." The Peacock Figures, with a calm, slightly mocking tone, responds, "This is a start. I might also convert the basement into a dungeon." He then turns to confront Shen, who looms in the shadows with a menacing presence, his horns prominent and his expression stern. Shen's voice returns, urging, "So your parents can rest in peace." The Peacock Figures, exuding arrogance, taunts Shen with self-aware humor, as indicated by the on-screen text: 'bros playing into it with self-aware jokes top-tier villain.' His voice shifts to a more serious, slightly pained tone as he says, "My parents hated me. Do you understand? They wronged me. And I will make it right." Shen's voice softens, trying to reason, "They loved you. They loved you so much that having to send you away killed them." The camera cuts between the two, emphasizing their psychological duel. The Peacock Figures then dramatically unfurls his ornate, eye-patterned feathers, revealing a confident, almost mocking demeanor, while the text overlay notes, 'leaving this scene here because I still love that they gave him actual depth.' With a dismissive, cold tone, the Peacock Figures states, "The dead exist in the past. And I must attend to the future." Shen responds with a growl, his anger palpable, and his voice becomes demanding, "Tell me what happened that night!" The Peacock Figures, feigning ignorance, asks, "What night?" Shen, intensely, repeats, "That night!" The Peacock Figures, with sarcastic realization, replies, "Ah, that night." Shen, urgently, presses, "Yes! We're talking about the same night, right?" The Peacock Figures continues to taunt him, saying, 'And yes, I'm gonna eat up parallels EVERY time.' The tension escalates as the Peacock Figures unleashes a red energy blast with a loud "YAAAH!", striking Shen and sending him flying backward in a dramatic explosion of debris, accompanied by whooshing, impact sounds, and Shen's pained "AHHH!". The scene ends with a close-up of the Peacock Figures, now fully transformed into a menacing figure with glowing red eyes, his feathers radiating power, as the text 'I am literally bitchless.' appears, underscoring his ruthless confidence. The environment is dark and foreboding, bathed in red light, with wooden structures and fire adding to the chaotic atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: In a dark, fiery environment bathed in intense red lighting and an orchestral score, Po lies on a wooden structure as a cannon fires a glowing projectile toward him with a distinct firing sound, while Shen, the horned boar-like antagonist, stands ominously in the shadows. The Peacock Figures, Lord Shen, appears with a theatrical, smug demeanor, taunting Po with exaggerated gestures and a sharp blade, accompanied by a metallic clang, his long neck and ornate feathers highlighted under the red glow as Po declares, "No more running, Shen." Lord Shen calmly replies, "So it seems," to which Po demands "Answers." Lord Shen, in a taunting voice, responds, "Oh, you want it enough so badly. You think knowing will heal you? Huh? Fill some crater in your soul." He then delivers a cruel blow, stating, "Well, here's your answer. Your parents didn't love you." As tension escalates, Lord Shen's voice becomes manic, declaring, "But here, let me heal you!" followed by Po's pained gasp. Lord Shen unleashes a powerful red energy blast with a loud whoosh and explosion, sending Po flying backward in a dramatic explosion of light and smoke, accompanied by Po's pained scream of "No!" and sounds of falling debris. On-screen text overlays offer humorous, meta-commentary like 'my god...' and 'bros playing into it with self-aware jokes top-tier villain,' adding irony to the intense action. More on-screen text like 'leaving this scene here because I still love that they gave him actual depth' and 'And yes, I'm gonna eat up parallels EVERY time' appears while the dramatic music continues, fading slightly. The sequence ends with a close-up of Lord Shen, his eyes wide and beak open in a manic expression, as text notes his descent into madness, suggesting his delusions are consuming him. His voice becomes chilling, almost whispering, as he continues his cruel taunt: "worthless, you'll have to be for your own flesh and blood not... not to love you. Even a little." The final shot shows a red energy trail arcing through the air, with additional on-screen text 'I am literally bitchless.' Po's desperate cry of "Lord Shen!" is cut off as the minute ends. Later, Shen, identified as the Peacock Figures, stands arrogantly on a platform, declaring with a confident voice, 'Nothing stands in my way,' wielding a blade and unleashing a powerful red energy blast, commanding 'Fire!' as it engulfs the screen in a blinding flash, sending Po flying backward in shock, his body contorted mid-air, accompanied by his grunts of pain. The environment is dark and ominous, with traditional Chinese architecture silhouetted against a stormy sky, and the red glow casting an intense, foreboding atmosphere. Shen, with his ornate feathers and theatrical demeanor, appears increasingly manic, questioning with frustration, 'How many times do I have to kill the same stinking panda?' After the blast, the scene cuts to Po lying dazed on a rooftop, then quickly transitions to him standing defiantly. An unseen ally's voice addresses Shen, stating, 'A panda stands between you and your destiny,' and defiantly challenges him, 'Prepare yourself for a hot serving of justice,' adding, 'Your reign of terror comes to an end.' Shen is then shown again, now flanked by two imposing Gorilla-Like Figures who stand as enforcers, adding to the tension. The camera pans across the darkened cityscape, revealing distant fires and smoke, emphasizing the scale of destruction. The ally's voice is heard again, confused, 'What? Disc of destruction!' followed by sounds of impacts and explosions. On-screen text appears intermittently, including 'WAR CRIMES.' and '(As many times as the plot demands, man)', adding a layer of meta-commentary and humor. The sequence ends with a close-up of Shen, his expression twisted in rage, as the red energy continues to pulse around him, while Po, after a pained groan, utters a resigned yet determined 'Okay,' hinting at further chaos to come, underscored by ominous music.

On-Screen Text.description: Intermittent white text overlays such as 'WAR CRIMES.' and '(As many times as the plot demands, man)', adding humorous, meta-commentary to the dramatic scene.

He speaks with an arrogant, frustrated voice, declaring 'Nothing stands in my way' and commanding 'Fire!', and later questions 'How many times do I have to kill the same stinking panda?'. He is addressed as 'Shen' by an adversary.

He is later seen standing defiantly on a rooftop, uttering a resigned 'Okay.' He is identified as 'the same stinking panda' by Shen and 'a panda' by an ally.

The camera cuts to Shen, the horned boar-like antagonist, standing ominously in the shadows. The focus then shifts to the Peacock Figures, Lord Shen, who appears with a theatrical, smug demeanor, taunting Po with exaggerated gestures and a sharp blade in hand, accompanied by a metallic clang. His long neck and ornate feathers are highlighted under the red glow as Po's determined voice declares, "No more running, Shen." Lord Shen, with a calm, slightly mocking tone, replies, "So it seems." Po then demands "Answers," to which Lord Shen, in a taunting, theatrical voice, responds, "Oh, you want it enough so badly. You think knowing will heal you? Huh? Fill some crater in your soul." Emphasizing his arrogance, Lord Shen delivers a cruel and deliberate blow, stating, "Well, here's your answer. Your parents didn't love you." Lord Shen then unleashes a powerful red energy blast with a loud whoosh and explosion, sending Po flying backward in a dramatic explosion of light and smoke, accompanied by Po's pained scream of "No!" and sounds of falling debris. His voice becomes chilling, almost whispering, as he continues his cruel taunt: "worthless, you'll have to be for your own flesh and blood not... not to love you. Even a little." Po's desperate cry of "Lord Shen!" is cut off as the minute ends.

event_blocks.summary: In a war-torn village, the Peacock Figures, a white peacock with ornate eye-patterned feathers, stands confidently amidst chaos, humorously labeled 'Shen at the 1997 Titanic premiere:' and smirking with an '*unbothered*' text overlay, emitting deep, calm, and slightly mocking laughter before stating, "As you wish" and "Let's finish this," as flames rage behind him. Po, the panda, is then struck by a red energy blast while lying on a wooden structure, sent flying backward with an expression of confusion turning to shock. The Peacock Figures descends into manic panic, screaming with wide eyes and flared feathers, his high-pitched, hysterical laughter and frantic cackles heard, accompanied by the text 'The panic in his voice-'. His voice becomes commanding, shouting "Again!", "Kill him! Somebody kill him!", and "Die!" amidst intense battle sounds and energy blasts. In a golden-hued sky, the Peacock Figures, now red-faced and furious, unleashes a powerful red energy blast with glowing feathers, punctuated by a panicked "No!" and desperate cries of "Keep firing!" Po reappears, standing defiantly on a rooftop, raising his fist in a gesture of challenge. The Peacock Figures recoils in terror, screaming 'Oh shit...' as he is struck by an unseen force, followed by a long, pained roar. A softer, vulnerable voice, identified as Po, is heard panting and asking "How did you... How did you do it?" and "How did you find...?", with a brief, calm instructional voice advising to "keep your elbows up and keep the shoulders loose." The final frames show the Peacock Figures in utter madness, eyes wide and feathers splayed, accompanied by a sharp, guttural sound, followed by a close-up of a Red-Eyed Creature with glowing eyes, suggesting a new, ominous threat, as the atmosphere shifts from chaotic fire to a golden, hazy sky, then to a dark, red-lit environment.

Red-Eyed Creature.description: A small, red-eyed animal, possibly a wolf or fox, with glowing eyes, seen in a close-up at the end, suggesting a new threat or transformation, adding to the ominous atmosphere.

Somebody kill him!", and "Die!", a panicked "No!", and desperate cries to "Keep firing!" as he unleashes a red energy blast, his feathers glowing and splayed in terror and madness, culminating in a long, pained roar.

The atmosphere shifts from chaotic fire to a golden, hazy sky, then to a dark, red-lit environment, emphasizing the escalating tension and the Peacock Figures' descent into madness.

event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit, red-hued environment, Shen, a horned boar-like creature, taunts Po, a panda, with a menacing voice, stating, "I took away your parents. Everything. I scarred you for life." Po, standing calmly against a warm, golden sky, responds with a resolute, "Scars heal." Shen dismissively retorts, "No, they don't. Wounds heal," to which Po questions, "Oh yeah, what do scars do? They fade, I guess?" Shen, frustrated, declares, "I don't care what scars do," while on-screen text humorously lists Shen's futile efforts as 'PAY 86', 'LIE 5', and 'BEG 12'. Po advises, "You gotta let go of that stuff from the past 'cause it just doesn't matter. The only thing that matters is what you choose to be now." Shen quietly concedes, "You're right," before explosively shouting, "Then I choose *this*!" This is followed by a loud blast and metallic clangs as the scene shifts to a rooftop battle. Po, struck by a red energy blast and sent flying, shouts "Shen!" in surprise, then shifts to defiance amidst intense martial arts combat sounds, including punches, kicks, and weapon clangs, accompanied by escalating orchestral music. Shen, in an aggressive stance with glowing feathers, unleashes another blast, while on-screen text states, 'Once again, in an attempt to avoid fate he ends up orchestrating it'. The environment transitions from dark reds to warm golds. The final frames show Shen in manic panic, screaming as he is overwhelmed by his own power, while Po stands resolute, raising his fist in challenge amidst the ongoing battle sounds.

On-Screen Text.description: Intermittent white text overlays such as 'Shen to Po at literally any given opportunity:', 'PAY 86', 'LIE 5', 'BEG 12', 'Once again, in an attempt to avoid fate he ends up orchestrating it', and 'I WANT THIS MF DEAD', adding humorous, meta-commentary and dramatic emphasis to the scene.

Shen.description: A large, horned boar-like creature with a red sash, initially seen in dimly lit, red-hued environments with an expression of intense anger and desperation. His voice is deep, menacing, and taunting, then becomes dismissive, angry, frustrated, and finally explosively aggressive, revealing his obsession with the past and his choice for destruction. He is later shown in a more aggressive stance with glowing feathers, and ultimately in a state of manic panic, screaming with his face contorted in rage and desperation as he is overwhelmed by his own power.

Po.description: A panda character, initially standing calmly against a warm, golden sky with a serene and composed face. His voice is calm, resolute, philosophical, and encouraging as he attempts to reason. He is later struck by a red energy blast, flying backward with an expression shifting from shock to defiance, and shouts "Shen!" in surprise and concern. He ultimately stands defiantly on a rooftop, raising his fist in challenge, his face calm and resolute against a golden sky.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a close-up of Shen, a large, horned boar-like creature, in a dimly lit, red-hued environment, his expression one of intense anger and desperation. His deep, menacing voice taunts, "I took away your parents. Everything. I scarred you for life." The scene cuts to Po, the panda, standing calmly against a warm, golden sky, his face serene and composed, responding with a resolute, "Scars heal." Shen, his voice dismissive, retorts, "No, they don't. Wounds heal." Po, slightly questioning, asks, "Oh yeah, what do scars do? They fade, I guess?" Shen, now angry and frustrated, declares, "I don't care what scars do." Text overlays appear, humorously highlighting Shen’s futile efforts with phrases like 'Shen to Po at literally any given opportunity:' followed by a game show-style scoreboard listing 'PAY 86', 'LIE 5', and 'BEG 12'. Po, with a philosophical and encouraging tone, advises, "You gotta let go of that stuff from the past 'cause it just doesn't matter. The only thing that matters is what you choose to be now." Shen, in a quiet, almost defeated voice, says, "You're right," before suddenly shouting, "Then I choose *this*!" This explosive declaration is accompanied by a loud blast and metallic clangs as the scene shifts to a rooftop battle. Po, struck by a red energy blast and sent flying, shouts "Shen!" in surprise and concern. He shifts from shock to defiance as intense, fast-paced martial arts combat sounds, including punches, kicks, and weapon clangs, erupt alongside dramatic, escalating orchestral music. Shen, now in a more aggressive stance with glowing feathers, unleashes another blast. On-screen text appears, stating 'Once again, in an attempt to avoid fate he ends up orchestrating it', underscoring the tragic irony. The environment transitions from dark, oppressive reds to warm, golden hues, symbolizing the clash between darkness and light. The final frames show Shen in a state of manic panic, screaming as he is overwhelmed by his own power, while Po stands resolute, raising his fist in challenge amidst the ongoing battle sounds.

event_blocks.summary: Po, the panda, lies defeated on a sinking wooden structure in a fiery environment, closing his eyes as a "Hmph!" and an energy charge sound precede a "WHOOSH!" and a red energy blast that sends him flying backward through debris and water in a massive explosion, accompanied by his pained grunt and the triumphant, maniacal laugh of the Peacock Figures. As the wreckage sinks into the water under a golden sky, distant explosions echo, followed by whistling sounds of fireworks launching and sparkling bursts filling the air with a majestic, hopeful orchestral score. A radiant display of fireworks erupts from the remains, forming a glowing peacock outline in the sky. The scene shifts to Po and other characters on a rooftop, looking up at the sky with awe and determination as the orchestral music swells, while on-screen text announces, 'The next KFP movie is coming out March!!!! GET READY YALL.' The final frame features a close-up of a red-eyed creature with glowing eyes, accompanied by a subtle, low growl, hinting at a new, ominous presence.
